label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. The question is asking if I follow these steps, do I return to the starting point. Let me go through each instruction one by one. First, "Always face forward." So my direction is fixed; I don't turn, I just face forward the whole time. That means any movement backward or sideways is relative to my forward-facing direction. Now, the steps: 1. Take 10 steps backward. Since I'm facing forward, moving backward would be in the opposite direction of my forward. So if I consider forward as North, backward would be South. Let's say each step is 1 unit. So 10 steps backward would take me 10 units South. 2. Next, take 7 steps backward again. So another 7 units South. Now total backward movement is 10 + 7 = 17 units South. So my position is 17 units South from start in the forward-backward axis. Then, moving sideways: 3. Take 8 steps right. Since I'm facing forward (North), right would be East. So 8 steps East. 4. Take 6 steps right again. Another 6 steps East. Total right movement is 8 + 6 = 14 units East. 5. Then take 3 steps left. Left from facing forward (North) would be West. So 3 steps West. Subtract that from the total East movement. So 14 - 3 = 11 units East. So now, in the forward-backward axis, I'm 17 units South of the start. In the sideways axis, I'm
